name: Camc
description_short: CAMC (Charleston Area Medical Center) is a major regional health system offering comprehensive hospital and clinical services including primary care, specialists, telemedicine, and advanced procedures like kidney transplants.
description_long:
Charleston Area Medical Center (CAMC), operating under the Vandalia Health brand, is a large integrated health system based in Charleston, West Virginia. The organization operates multiple hospitals and clinical facilities serving the Vandalia North Region and surrounding areas, employing thousands of healthcare professionals across hundreds of different roles. CAMC provides a broad spectrum of healthcare services ranging from routine primary care to specialized surgical procedures and transplant medicine. The organization emphasizes accessibility, safety, and quality in its care delivery model.

CAMC's service portfolio includes primary care physician networks, specialist referrals, telemedicine through a 24/7 Care app offering virtual visits, pediatric care, heart and vascular services, and advanced surgical programs. The organization offers online appointment scheduling through digital platforms accessible via smartphone or laptop. A significant specialized service is the CAMC Kidney Transplant Center, which has performed over 1,700 kidney transplants since 1987 and offers comprehensive transplant evaluation, surgery, and post-operative care through a multidisciplinary team including transplant surgeons, nephrologists, dietitians, social workers, and financial coordinators. The program is affiliated with Cleveland Clinic Foundation.

What distinguishes CAMC is its combination of scale as a regional medical center with specialized expertise in transplant medicine. The kidney transplant program specifically demonstrates sustained clinical outcomes matching national averages for one-year post-transplant patient and graft survival rates. The organization actively promotes both deceased donor and living donor kidney transplants, with infrastructure for UNOS waitlist management and multiple listing coordination. CAMC maintains Magnet® designation for nursing excellence and has achieved Center of Excellence status in robotic gynecologic surgery.

CAMC is a healthcare provider, not a consumer finance or credit services company. While the organization may offer financial counseling to transplant patients and likely has financial payment options for uninsured or underinsured patients, it does not operate as a payday alternative lender, credit union, or financial services provider. The current categorization as a "payday-alternatives" company is fundamentally incorrect and reflects a data classification error rather than actual business operations.
services: Specialty physician care across multiple medical fields
Kidney transplant surgery and management (deceased donor and living donor)
24/7 telemedicine virtual visits through 24/7 Care app
Post-transplant follow-up care and medications management
Transplant patient education (diet, medications, living donors)
pros: Kidney Transplant Center with 30+ years of experience and over 1,700 successful transplants performed since 1987
Multidisciplinary transplant care team including surgeons, nephrologists, coordinators, dietitians, social workers, and financial coordinators
24/7 virtual care app enabling convenient telemedicine access for illnesses and injuries outside normal business hours
Online appointment scheduling through digital platforms accessible via smartphone and laptop
Affiliation with Cleveland Clinic Foundation, one of the nation's leaders in transplant medicine
Magnet® designation for nursing excellence demonstrating quality nursing care standards
Kidney transplant outcomes matching national averages for one-year post-transplant survival rates
cons: No information provided about patient financial assistance programs, payment plans, or insurance coverage details on publicly available website pages
Limited details on transplant wait times or specific acceptance criteria for kidney transplant program beyond general mention of comprehensive medical evaluation
Website content does not address out-of-pocket costs, billing practices, or financial burden of advanced procedures like kidney transplants
No information about serving uninsured or low-income patients or scholarship/assistance programs
Geographic limitation to Vandalia North Region and Charleston, West Virginia area for most services
best_for: Patients with end-stage renal disease seeking kidney transplant evaluation and surgery in West Virginia or surrounding regions
Adults requiring specialized heart and vascular care or pediatric services through established regional health system
Patients preferring telemedicine options for routine illness and injury assessment through 24/7 virtual visits
Healthcare job seekers looking for employment at large integrated medical center with hundreds of professional positions
diagnosis: CAMC is a regional health system providing comprehensive medical care including advanced transplant services, not a consumer finance or credit alternative lender. The categorization as "payday-alternatives" is factually incorrect and should be recategorized as a healthcare provider. CAMC is appropriate for patients seeking kidney transplants, specialized medical care, or telemedicine services in the West Virginia region.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
